overview.dataFreshnessNote: All 3 sequences remain paused. Metrics are unchanged from Report #002 (May 22). Fresh step-level data pulled from platform on June 18, 2026.

periodDelta.note: Deltas are vs Report #1 baseline. All 3 sequences paused since May 22.

matchback.title: Inspection Matchback Analysis
matchback.summary: A matchback was performed comparing 188 actual home inspections against all 6 AAA Pro sequences (1,715 total contacts). Result: zero matches.
matchback.verdict: The sequences have never sent outreach to anyone on the inspection list. These clients came in through a different door entirely — agents, referrals, search, walk-ins, etc.
matchback.keyFinding: The Home Inspection Buyers Sequence — the only one targeting actual home buyers — is populated with B2B/professional contacts (LinkedIn-sourced, company emails). Meanwhile the inspection list is 147 unique personal emails. These are fundamentally different populations. The sequence list came from a B2B/professional source (likely Apollo or LinkedIn scraping), while actual inspection clients are real consumers who found AAA through other channels.

sequences.name: Home Inspection Buyers Sequence
sequences.note: 585 contacts queued. Paused per client request. 64.44% open rate on 225 sent.

stepMetrics.note: 21 SMS failures. 33 sent but 0 delivered — SMS integration may need review.

sequences.name: Real Estate Agent Referral Sequence
sequences.note: Highest open rate (91.73%). Paused per client request.

sequences.name: Real Estate Agent Referral Sequence - Apollo
sequences.note: Strongest click engagement (4.15% CR). Apollo-sourced. Paused per client request.

insights.title: Matchback: Zero Inspection Attribution from Sequences
insights.detail: 188 actual inspections were matched against all 6 sequences (1,715 contacts). Result: 0 matches. The sequence contacts (B2B/professional, company emails) and inspection clients (personal emails — Gmail, Yahoo, AOL) are fundamentally different populations. These clients came through agents, referrals, search, and walk-ins.

insights.title: Exceptional Open Rates Across All Sequences
insights.detail: 607 unique opens across 732 total sends. Original referral leads at 91.73%, Apollo variant at 90.46%, and Home Inspection Buyers at 64.44% — all well above industry benchmarks.

insights.title: Apollo-Sourced Contacts Show Strongest Click Engagement
insights.detail: The Apollo variant delivers a 4.15% click rate (10 unique clicks) vs 0.75% on the original referral (2 clicks). Step-level data shows clicks concentrated in Email 1 (4) and Email 2 (4), tapering to 2 in Email 3.

insights.title: SMS Step Shows Delivery Issues
insights.detail: Home Inspection Buyers Step 1 (SMS): 80 executed, 21 failed (26.25% failure rate). 33 SMS sent but 0 recorded as delivered. SMS integration may need troubleshooting if reactivated.

insights.title: 585 Contacts Queued — Largest Untapped Pipeline
insights.detail: 665 total members in Home Inspection Buyers with 585 not yet started — 88% of the sequence is untouched. However, matchback shows these are B2B contacts, not consumer home buyers.

insights.title: Zero Replies Across All Sequences
insights.detail: Despite strong open rates, no replies have been received across any sequence. Combined with the matchback findings, this suggests the contact lists may not align with the target audience.

notes.clientRequest: All 3 sequences paused per client request. This is the final report for this engagement.
notes.bookingDisclaimer: Bookings are not currently tracked in the MMai system. All booking metrics show 0 — this does not mean no meetings occurred, only that they are not tracked here.
notes.dataAvailability: Fresh step-level metrics pulled from platform on June 18, 2026. No LinkedIn/Prosp campaigns.
